The Archiver v2.1.0
GitHub

Your Web. Your Archive.
Your Server.

Self-hosted, plugin-based web content archiver.
One Docker container. One command. Done.

Get Started View Source
System Capabilities

Everything you need to archive the web

01

Plugin-Based Downloads

Drop-in TypeScript plugins matched by URL pattern. First match handles the download automatically.

02

Real-Time Dashboard

Live queue monitoring, download stats, history, and server console via Server-Sent Events.

03

File Browser

Browse, organize, rename, move, copy, and download your archived content directly from the UI.

04

REST API

Simple POST or GET endpoint. Integrate with anything that can make an HTTP request.

05

Multi-Device

Archive from Apple Shortcuts, browser bookmarklets, Tasker, IFTTT, n8n, or any automation tool.

06

Push Notifications

Get notified via ntfy.sh when downloads complete or fail. Configure from the Settings page.

Next.js 16 / React 19 / SQLite / Drizzle ORM / SSE / Docker
Quick Start

Up and running in 30 seconds

01

Run the container

Single Docker command. No Redis, no external database, no orchestration.

02

Open the dashboard

Navigate to localhost:3000 to access the full web interface.

03

Archive a URL

Submit via the UI, REST API, Apple Shortcut, or bookmarklet. A matching plugin handles the rest.

terminal
$ docker run -d \
-p 3000:3000 \
-v ./data:/data \
-v ./downloads:/downloads \
-v ./plugins:/plugins \
ghcr.io/pauljoda/the-archiver:latest
# Archive a URL via the API
$ curl -X POST \
http://localhost:3000/api/download \
-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
-d '{"url": "https://example.com/content"}'
Plugin System

Extend with TypeScript plugins

  • TypeScript-first with full type safety
  • URL pattern matching — first match wins
  • Built-in helpers for HTML, file I/O, strings
  • Install via UI upload or filesystem
  • Hot-reload without server restart
  • Declare custom settings per plugin
my-plugin/index.ts
import { definePlugin } from "../../src/plugins/types";
export default definePlugin({
name: "My Plugin",
urlPatterns: ["https://example.com"],
async download(context) {
const { url, helpers, logger } = context;
// Fetch and parse HTML
const html = await helpers.html.fetchPage(url);
const $ = helpers.html.parse(html);
// Download files
await helpers.io.downloadFile(imageUrl, path);
return { success: true };
},
});
The Archiver
v2.1.0

Self-hosted web content archiver.
One container. Zero dependencies.
